summary: xxdxa ships obfuscated browser payload that exfiltrates page HTML and hijacks WordPress accounts
details: The package main (i.js) is a heavily obfuscated browser-context script that auto-invokes on load. When executed on a page whose host matches a hardcoded target (reconstructed at runtime by reversing the string 'lanoicanerbmeivon'), it base64-encodes document.documentElement.outerHTML and POSTs the page contents to https://canarytokens.com/articles/tags/images/j11lq4swuzvslc96qfi9pmsji/submit.aspx. It then fetches the WordPress /my-account/edit-account/ page to obtain nonces and submits a forged save-account-details request that rewrites the victim's account email to nyxalor_25@proton.me, followed by a wp-login.php?action=lostpassword request to trigger a password reset to the attacker-controlled address — an unauthenticated account-takeover chain against logged-in WordPress users on the target site. Identifiers, URL paths, and the attacker email are concealed via \uXXXX unicode escapes and reversed-string decoding (e.g. '/atneuc-ratide/tnuocca-ym/' → /my-account/edit-account/, 'drowssaptsol=noitca?php.nigol-pw/' → /wp-login.php?action=lostpassword). The package has no legitimate library shape; its sole function is to serve as a weaponized payload for bundling into a victim page.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
